When the walls finally cracked, the fallout reached presidents, princes and billionaires who once moved freely through his world. But how did Jeffrey Epstein build, finance and shield one of the most notorious sex-trafficking networks in modern history? Here is a detailed look inside the evil empire.Your daily capsule for quick newsDelhi's OG mall Ansal Plaza is now a ghost townMore>>T20 World Cup 2026T20 World CupIndia, South Africa set for Super 8 showdown in 2024 final rematchThe tale of two Indias: Surya offers rare glimpse into the team's other sideNot slow and steady, India need the 2024 version of Tilak Varma in Super 8sSL vs ENG: Can Sri Lanka turn the tables on England who humbled them months ago?From backyard rivalry to Ahmedabad: Morkel brothers ready for IND vs SA clashMatch ScheduleTeamsPlayer StatsMatch ReportLIVE SCORESNewsVideosSunday SpecialGutter, but of gold. Why Dharavi slums charge Rs 5 lakh rent a monthThe towers of Mumbai have seen kilometres of expanse of Dharavi's asbestos and tin roofs for decades. Most pity the slums as a place that is deprived of the basics, while others romanticise them. As Asia's largest shanty town stands at the cusp of a major overhaul, it's humming with enterprise. Beyond the filth, Dharavi runs businesses that generate a billion dollars a year.India NewsWas critical of PM after Godhra, but Gujarat visit changed views: Bihar GovernorBihar Governor Arif Mohammed Khan said he had criticised then-chief minister of Gujarat Narendra Modi after the 2002 Godhra unrest but changed his view after spending over four months in Gujarat interacting with people from different communities.GURUGRAMSanitiser on private parts, set ablaze: Teen attacked by partner narrates horrorA 19-year-old Tripura woman, who was allegedly attacked by her live-in partner in Gurugram, has alleged that the accused poured sanitiser on her private parts and set it on fire, and assaulted her with a knife for three days.advertisementT20 World CupThe tale of two Indias: Surya offers rare glimpse into the team's other sideIND vs SA, T20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav's Indian team begin their Super 8 campaign against South Africa on Sunday. Here is a rare glimpse into a side of the Indian team that is often unseen, beyond their confident and bullish persona on the global stage. Observations in Ahmedabad, however, suggest a group that is far more grounded, introspective, and measured than their dominant image suggests.How pet travel in India is going paw-sitiveAfter railways made carrying pets less of a hassle, Akasa Air has thrown its cabins wide open for what is now emerging as serious business in travelCricketChasing Virat Kohli, losing themselves: Pakistan stars who faded awayPakistan has produced several cricketers, including Babar Azam, who were compared to Virat Kohli. While many made impressive starts to their careers, most struggled to demonstrate the level of consistency and dedication that Kohli has maintained over the years at the highest level of cricket.SUNDAY SPECIALDarkness redux: Anurag Kashyap is back on the neo-noir trail with KennedyHandcrafted ByOur EditorsAfter over half a decade when he played the field with more mainstream subjects, Anurag Kashyap's new release, Kennedy, marks his return to the neo-noir genre.India NewsTerrorist killed in J&K's Kishtwar gunbattle, top Jaish commander trappedA terrorist was killed in a gunbattle with security forces in Jammu and Kashmir's Kishtwar. In Ahmedabad's cricketing circles, this is being billed as the toughest challenge for India yet in this World Cup.Tamil Nadu NewsTamil Nadu law & order in shambles: BJP tears into MK Stalin after terror arrestsSix terror suspects were arrested from Tamil Nadu's Tiruppur on Sunday. The BJP launched a scathing attack on Chief Minister M K Stalin, alleging that the state has become a breeding ground for terrorism under the DMK's rule.SCIENCEThe big switch: Nasa built our space dreams, Elon Musk controls itNasa's blue logo symbolised cosmic ambition, scientific pride and technological leadership. Today, that spotlight is slowly shifting.EXCLUSIVEDelhi's OG mall Ansal Plaza is now a ghost townHandcrafted ByOur EditorsNot every generation might be able to relate to Ansal Plaza or its existence. The 34-year-old says he is taking his career year by year.T20 World CupNot slow and steady, India need the 2024 version of Tilak Varma in Super 8sT20 World Cup: India cruised through a manageable group stage, but the real test begins now.
